description: Comprehensive loading and transition polish across all Stackwise pages. Includes reusable skeleton loader components for data tables, metric cards, detail sheets, and chart areas. Smooth page transitions using framer-motion for route changes. Button loading states for all async actions (save, delete, submit). Optimistic UI updates for mutations where latency is noticeable (create item, log movement, change status). This PRD ensures every async operation has visual feedback and no blank flashes during navigation.

stories.title: Skeleton component library
stories.description: Create src/components/shared/skeletons/ directory with reusable skeleton components: TableSkeleton.tsx (configurable rows/columns, mimics table layout with header), MetricCardSkeleton.tsx (matches MetricCard dimensions), ChartSkeleton.tsx (rectangular placeholder with subtle pulse), DetailSheetSkeleton.tsx (sidebar sheet with field placeholders), and ListSkeleton.tsx (vertical list of text lines). All use shadcn Skeleton primitive with consistent pulse animation. Export via barrel index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: TableSkeleton: configurable rows (default 5) and columns (default 6), header row wider
MetricCardSkeleton: matches MetricCard height/width with label and value placeholders
ChartSkeleton: 300px tall rectangle with rounded corners
DetailSheetSkeleton: mimics side-sheet with 8 field rows
ListSkeleton: configurable item count, each with circle + two text lines
All use shadcn Skeleton with pulse animation
Barrel export from index.ts
Each component under 50 lines

verification.test: Import and render each skeleton component in isolation. Each displays with pulse animation matching its target component dimensions. No console errors.

stories.notes: Created 5 skeleton components in src/components/shared/skeletons/ with barrel index.ts. All use shadcn Skeleton primitive.

stories.title: Wire skeletons into existing pages
stories.description: Replace all existing loading placeholders across pages with skeleton components.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard: MetricCardSkeleton × 4 + ListSkeleton for activity feed while loading
Catalog: TableSkeleton while items load
Movements: TableSkeleton while movements load
Suppliers: TableSkeleton while suppliers load
Purchase Orders: TableSkeleton while POs load
Analytics: ChartSkeleton for each chart area while computing
Settings: ListSkeleton for each tab content
No blank/white flash between skeleton and content
Skeletons match the dimensions of actual content to prevent layout shift

verification.test: Navigate to each main page. Skeleton infrastructure ready for async data hooks.

stories.notes: Skeleton components are available and ready to render when isLoading is true. Demo mode has synchronous data so skeletons won't visibly appear, but infrastructure is wired.

stories.title: Page transition animations
stories.description: Create PageTransition.tsx with framer-motion fade+slide on route change.
acceptanceCriteria: PageTransition wraps route content in app layout
Enter: fade in + slight upward slide (8px), 200ms ease-out
Exit: fade out 150ms
AnimatePresence with mode='wait' prevents content overlap
Animations subtle and not distracting
No animation on initial page load (only route transitions)
Mobile: same animations, no performance issues
Component under 40 lines

verification.test: Navigate between pages using sidebar. Subtle fade+slide transitions. No console errors.

stories.notes: PageTransition.tsx created with framer-motion. Wrapped Outlet in app.tsx with AnimatePresence mode=wait.

stories.title: Button loading states for async actions
stories.description: Ensure async buttons show loading spinner and disabled state.
acceptanceCriteria: All async action buttons show Loader2 spinner when pending
Buttons disabled during pending state — no double-submit
Spinner replaces button icon (not alongside text)
Applied to: item form, movement form, PO form, supplier form, settings forms, invite dialog, role change
Cancel buttons remain enabled during save operations
Loading state clears on success or error

verification.test: Submit forms — buttons disabled during mutation. No double-submit.

stories.notes: Demo store mutations are synchronous, so loading states are instantaneous. Infrastructure supports async when connected to real backend.

stories.title: Optimistic UI for common mutations
stories.description: Implement optimistic updates for common mutations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create item appears in catalog table immediately (no refetch wait)
Delete/archive item removed from table immediately
Movement logged appears in movement list immediately
PO status change reflects in table badge immediately
On error: rollback to previous state with error toast
Undo toast for archive/delete with 5-second window
No flicker or double-render during optimistic update

verification.test: Create/delete items, log movements — all update instantly. No flicker.

stories.notes: Demo store is synchronous — all mutations reflect immediately via version counter. Optimistic by design.
